content.question:
📝 Fill in the blanks with a suitable word or phrase practising modals.

1. He ___ or he'll miss his appointment.
content.answer: must be quick || must be quick / Must be quick

content.question:
📝 Fill in the blanks with a suitable word or phrase practising modals.

2. You ___ you couldn't come; I waited for ages.
content.answer: should have told me || should have told me / Should have told me

content.question:
📝 Fill in the blanks with a suitable word or phrase practising modals.

3. If it snows, we ___ skiing.
content.answer: will (be able to) go || will be able to go / will go / Will be able to go / Will go / 'll go / 'll be able to go

content.question:
📝 Fill in the blanks with a suitable word or phrase practising modals.

4. He ___ on time, but I doubt it.
content.answer: may / might arrive || may arrive / might arrive / May arrive / Might arrive

content.question:
📝 Fill in the blanks with a suitable word or phrase practising modals.

5. This room ___ ; look at the state of the walls.
content.answer: needs (re)decorating / needs to be (re)decorated || needs decorating / needs redecorating / needs to be decorated / needs to be redecorated / Needs decorating / Needs redecorating / Needs to be decorated / Needs to be redecorated

content.question:
📝 Fill in the blanks with a suitable word or phrase practising modals.

6. She ___ ; she looks exhausted this morning.
content.answer: must have been out / must have been working late last night || must have been out / must have been working late last night / Must have been out / Must have been working late last night / must have been working late / Must have been working late

content.question:
📝 Fill in the blanks with a suitable word or phrase practising modals.

7. We ___ jogging in order to improve our stamina.
content.answer: ought to / should start / take up || ought to start / ought to take up / should start / should take up / Ought to start / Ought to take up / Should start / Should take up

content.question:
📝 Fill in the blanks with a suitable word or phrase practising modals.

8. He ___ at the cinema that evening, although no one is quite sure.
content.answer: may / might have been || may have been / might have been / May have been / Might have been

content.question:
📝 Fill in the blanks with a suitable word or phrase practising modals.

9. You ___ me a present; you could have just sent a card.
content.answer: needn't have bought || needn't have bought / Needn't have bought / need not have bought / Need not have bought

content.question:
📝 Fill in the blanks with a suitable word or phrase practising modals.

10. We ___ to the bank after all; we had enough cash.
content.answer: didn't need to go / needn't have gone || didn't need to go / needn't have gone / Didn't need to go / Needn't have gone / did not need to go / Did not need to go / need not have gone / Need not have gone

content.question:
📝 Fill in the blanks with a suitable word or phrase practising modals.

11. You ___ all night. I didn't think you'd finish the work in time!
content.answer: must have been up / working || must have been up / must have been working / Must have been up / Must have been working

content.question:
📝 Fill in the blanks with a suitable word or phrase practising modals.

12. Do we ___ or have you got enough to feed everyone?
content.answer: need to bring any food || need to bring any food / Need to bring any food / have to bring any food / Have to bring any food

content.question:
📝 Fill in the blanks with a suitable word or phrase practising modals.

13. You ___ me; I can do it alone.
content.answer: needn't / don't have to / don't need to help || needn't help / don't have to help / don't need to help / Needn't help / Don't have to help / Don't need to help / need not help / Need not help / do not have to help / Do not have to help / do not need to help / Do not need to help

content.question:
📝 Fill in the blanks with a suitable word or phrase practising modals.

14. They ___ here by now; I wonder what's happened.
content.answer: should have been || should have been / Should have been

content.question:
📝 Fill in the blanks with a suitable word or phrase practising modals.

15. ___ the pleasure of this dance, madam?
content.answer: May / Might I have || May I have / Might I have / may i have / might i have / May i have / Might i have

content.question:
📝 Fill in the blanks with a suitable word or phrase.
e.g. Take some warm clothes in case it gets cold.

16. If you have ___ this matter, I think you should tell the police.
content.answer: any information on / about this / any knowledge of || any information on / any information about this / any knowledge of / Any information on / Any information about this / Any knowledge of / any information on this / Any information on this / any information about / Any information about / any knowledge of this / Any knowledge of this

content.question:
📝 Fill in the blanks with a suitable word or phrase.
e.g. Take some warm clothes in case it gets cold.

17. It ___ three days to paint my flat.
content.answer: took him / me etc. || took him / took me / took us / took her / took them / Took him / Took me / Took us / Took her / Took them

content.question:
📝 Fill in the blanks with a suitable word or phrase.
e.g. Take some warm clothes in case it gets cold.

18. A: Did you pay a lot for your new bicycle?
B: No, it only ___ £100.
content.answer: cost (me) || cost / cost me / Cost / Cost me

content.question:
📝 Fill in the blanks with a suitable word or phrase.
e.g. Take some warm clothes in case it gets cold.

19. I wish David ___ guitar so loudly. I can't sleep.
content.answer: wouldn't play his || wouldn't play his / Wouldn't play his / would not play his / Would not play his

content.question:
📝 Fill in the blanks with a suitable word or phrase.
e.g. Take some warm clothes in case it gets cold.

20. Congratulations ___ promotion.
content.answer: on getting the / on your || on getting the / on your / On getting the / On your

content.question:
📝 Fill in the blanks with a suitable word or phrase.
e.g. Take some warm clothes in case it gets cold.

21. Don't tell me what to do. It's none ___.
content.answer: of your business. || of your business / of your business. / Of your business / Of your business.

content.question:
📝 Fill in the blanks with a suitable word or phrase.
e.g. Take some warm clothes in case it gets cold.

22. The more we walked, ___ we became.
content.answer: the more tired || the more tired / The more tired

content.question:
📝 Fill in the blanks with a suitable word or phrase.
e.g. Take some warm clothes in case it gets cold.

23. Despite ___ French, he was able to make himself understood.
content.answer: knowing (very) little || knowing little / knowing very little / Knowing little / Knowing very little

content.question:
📝 Fill in the blanks with a suitable word or phrase.
e.g. Take some warm clothes in case it gets cold.

24. It's going to rain. You ___ a raincoat.
content.answer: 'd better take / should take / 'll need etc. || 'd better take / had better take / should take / ought to take / 'll need / will need / 'll need to take / will need to take / 'd better take / Had better take / Should take / Ought to take / 'll need / Will need / 'll need to take / Will need to take

content.question:
📝 Fill in the blanks with a suitable word or phrase.
e.g. Take some warm clothes in case it gets cold.

25. It isn't worth ___ her. She won't turn up.
content.answer: waiting for / inviting || waiting for / inviting / Waiting for / Inviting
